title: Individual factors in changing health beliefs
paperName: Paper 3 - Specialist Options: Approaches, Issues and Debates
summary: 9990 Health — locus of control, self-efficacy, and stages of change in health behaviour.
learningObjectives: Explain Rotter's locus of control in health contexts.
Outline Bandura's self-efficacy and health behaviour.
Apply Prochaska & DiClemente stages of change model.
Evaluate why some individuals resist health messages.

simpleExplanation.title: Why some people change — and others don't
simpleExplanation.summary: Health belief change depends on whether people feel in control, believe they can succeed, and are ready to act — not just on receiving information.
steps: Internal LOC — 'my behaviour affects my health'.
Relapse — normal part of behaviour change.

sections.content: **Individual health beliefs** (3.5.3) completes Health Psychology. **Stages of change** model is essential.
